response.justification: The candidate brings 15+ years of strong software engineering experience with directly relevant developer-tooling work (a D test runner 'Trial', the 'Fluent-Asserts' library, CI/CD setup, GitLab/artifactory infrastructure, and recent MCP code generators), plus some mobile exposure (iOS and Apple TV apps). However, this is a people-leadership role and the resume shows no evidence of formal people management—no direct reports, performance reviews, one-on-ones, or org-change leadership. All titles are individual-contributor (Staff/Senior Engineer). The technical fit is good, but the core management requirement is unmet, making this a borderline case worth a screening call to probe leadership experience.
strengths: Deep developer-tools and testing-infrastructure background (test runner, assertion library, CI/CD, DevOps)
15+ years across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded; exceeds the 8-year minimum
Demonstrated mentoring/teaching (dojos, multi-module Selenium course, workshops) signaling leadership potential
concerns: No evidence of formal people management (no reports, 1:1s, performance expectations)—the role's core requirement
No stated experience leading organizational change or owning team roadmaps
Limited mobile-platform depth relative to a 'major mobile platform ecosystem' focus
